meta.name: malformed-layout
meta.description: Language-neutral expected outcomes for malformed-container fixtures. Independent implementations MUST refuse these capsules: stage 'open' means the reader rejects the container before verification (by error, exception, or fail-closed result, per the host language's idiom); the default verify stage means the capsule opens but verification reports ok=false.
meta.no_warranty: Conformance fixtures only; not production templates or advice.

reasons.missing_required_file: a required top-level file (manifest.json or provenance/envelope.json) is absent
reasons.invalid_json: a required JSON document does not parse
reasons.duplicate_entry: two ZIP entries share the same name (parser differential)
reasons.unsafe_path: an entry name is absolute, contains a parent-traversal segment, or a NUL byte (checked on the RAW stored name, before any library-level sanitization)
reasons.unsupported_compression: an entry uses a compression method other than STORED
reasons.symlink_entry: an entry's Unix mode bits mark it as a symlink
reasons.entry_count_limit: RESERVED: archive exceeds the 10,000-entry cap (no checked-in fixture yet; a >10k-entry archive is ~1 MiB of registry weight, deferred until the limits profile is normative)
reasons.total_size_limit: RESERVED: archive exceeds the 1 GiB total-uncompressed cap (cannot be checked in; covered by per-lane unit tests)

notes: The 'reason' field is normative: an implementation must reject the fixture for the named reason category. Exact error strings are implementation-defined.

missing-chain: the failing chain-file case surfaces differently per lane today (JS: content_index only, chain deferred; Python/Rust: chain error plus content_index). The registry therefore pins only the content_index area; chain-area treatment is flagged for the verifier result-vocabulary normalization (TODO P1).
